segments.s003-g9: نخستین introduces a temporal opening 'at first'. که here is the subordinating conjunction 'when/that' linking the clause that follows. آتش is the subject and به جنبش دمید is a verbal phrase: به + noun (جنبش, 'into motion') + past verb دمید ('breathed/blew'), literally 'breathed into motion.' The whole segment is a temporal subordinate clause ('when the fire breathed into motion').
segments.s003-g10: ز گرمیش = ز (poetic 'from'/'because of') + گرمِ -یش ('its warmth'), so 'from its heat'. پس here is 'then/afterwards'. خشکی آمد پدید is a two-part verbal expression: آمد ('came') + پدید ('appeared/manifested'), a common poetic collocation meaning 'came into being / appeared.' The order is slightly poetic but functions as 'then dryness appeared.'
segments.s003-g11: و زان پس is a sequence marker: و 'and' + زان ('from that' / 'thereupon') + پس ('after/then'), giving 'and then/thereafter'. ز آرام سردی is 'from (a) calm/gentle coldness' where آرام qualifies سردی. نمود is the past verb 'appeared'/'showed itself', so the clause means 'and thereafter a gentle coldness appeared.'
segments.s003-g12: ز سردی = 'from the coldness'. همان ('that same' / 'that very') picks up the previously mentioned coldness. باز‌تری is used as an abstract noun/adjective meaning 'greater openness' or 'openness/expanse' and فزود is the past verb 'increased'/'grew'. Word order places the thing increased (باز‌تری) before the verb, producing 'from the coldness that same openness increased.'
segments.s003-g13: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. این چار گوهر is the noun phrase 'these four essences/elements' (چار here = 'four', گوهر = 'essence/element'). به جای آمدند is a verbal phrase meaning 'came to their place / took their positions' (به جای = 'to the place/position', آمدند = plural past 'they came').
segments.s003-g14: ز بهر is an idiomatic combination meaning 'for the sake of' (ز here carrying 'because of/for' nuance and بهر = 'for'). سپنجی modifies سرای and means 'spongy' or 'sponge-like'. سرای here denotes a place/abode (the provided lemma senses are not literal here), and آمدند is again 'they came' — so the phrase means 'they came for the sake of a spongy house/abode' or 'to form a spongy dwelling.'
segments.s003-g15: گهرها is the plural noun 'the essences/elements'. یک اندر دگر is an idiomatic phrase 'one within another' (یک = one, اندر = in/within, دگر = other/another). ساخته is the past participle/simple past 'made'/'constructed', so the segment says 'the essences were made one inside another.'
segments.s003-g16: ز هر گونه = 'from every kind/type' (ز = 'from', هر = 'each/every', گونه = 'kind'). گردن برافراخته is a nominal phrase 'a neck raised up' or 'with necks uplifted' (گردن = neck, برافراخته = erect/raised), describing that from each kind a lofty neck was set up. The order is poetic but straightforward: source + result (a raised neck).
segments.s003-g1: از آغاز is a prepositional phrase 'from the beginning' (از + آغاز). باید که is a modal construction 'it is necessary/you must that...' where که introduces the clause; دانی is the present/subjunctive/optative form of دانستن used after باید که ('you should/ought to know'). درست follows the verb as an adverb meaning 'correctly/properly.'
segments.s003-g2: This is a noun phrase giving what one must know: سر مایه گوهران = 'the chief/substantial matter of the essences' (سر مایه = principal/substance). از نخست is a locative/temporal phrase 'from the first / originally.' Word order is poetic: noun cluster + modifying time phrase.
segments.s003-g3: که introduces a subordinate content clause 'that ...'. یزدان is the subject 'God.' ز is the poetic preposition 'from/out of,' linking ناچیز چیز ('an insignificant thing' / 'nothing') as the object. آفرید is the past tense verb 'created / made.'
segments.s003-g4: بدان is the imperative/hortative 'know this,' formed from the prefix ب- + دان (see senses). تا here is the purpose/complementizer 'so that / in order that,' and it governs the following clause. توانایی is the noun 'ability/power.' آرد is a present/subjunctive 3rd-singular (classical/poetic form) used after تا to give purpose 'may bring,' and پدید is the verbal complement 'into being / forth' (so آرد پدید = 'bring forth').
segments.s003-g5: A nominal identification: 'سر مایه گوهران' = 'the prime/substantial matters of the essences.' این چهار ('these four') points to the four items being named; Persian allows this demonstrative to follow the head noun in poetic style.
segments.s003-g6: برآورده is a participial/predicative form meaning 'brought forth / produced' and here predicates over 'این چهار.' بی- is a negative prefix 'without' forming compounds (بی‌رنج = 'without toil/pain', بی‌روزگار = 'without misfortune/ill-fate'); و coordinates the two negatives. Together they describe how the four came into being (i.e. 'without toil and without misfortune').
segments.s003-g7: یکی is an indefinite 'one / a certain,' introducing an element. آتشی is the noun 'fire' with the adjectival suffix -ی ('fiery' or 'a fire'); بر شده is a compound verbal phrase 'has risen / was raised' (بر + شده), and تابناک is an adjective 'radiant / shining' qualifying the fire. Word order is poetic: indefinite + noun + verbal participle + adjective.
segments.s003-g8: میان آب و باد is a locative phrase 'between water and wind.' از بر is a poetic double prepositional expression (از + بر) meaning roughly 'from / upon / out of' and governs تیره خاک 'the dark earth' (تیره modifies خاک). The whole segment gives the spatial setting of the risen fire.
segments.s003-g49: This is a nominal predicate clause with the copula est (است). The sequence places the predicate phrase 'چنین' ('thus') before the verb: literally 'thus is the outcome of the world's affairs.' 'فرجام کار جهان' is a noun phrase (فرجام + کار + جهان) meaning 'the outcome/end of the world's affairs' where the genitive relationship (the world's affairs) is implicit in the juxtaposition; the present-tense copula است simply links that noun phrase to 'چنین.'
segments.s003-g50: نداند is a classical/poetic present-tense negative form meaning 'does not know.' The verb precedes the (indefinite) pronoun کسی, a common poetic word order; because of the negation, کسی here yields the universal negative sense 'no one.' آشکار و نهان are adjectives used substantively — 'the manifest and the hidden' — functioning as the things that are not known. The coordinating و simply joins the two contrasted realms.
segments.s003-g25: The sentence opens with چو used temporally ('when'); the repeated چون before each noun functions as a series of similes ('like the sea, like the mountain, like the plain...'). The coordinator و simply links the list items. راغ is an archaic poetic noun here ('longing' or 'desire') used as one more image in the series.
segments.s003-g26: زمین is the topical subject and شد is the past of شدن ('became'). به + کردار expresses manner ('in the manner/likeness of'); روشن modifies چراغ ('a bright lamp'). Word order places the result clause after the list of similes.
segments.s003-g27: ببالید is a past-tense verb meaning 'grew/was lifted' (here applied to کوه). The clause is paratactic: two short actions are juxtaposed (کوه بالید / آب‌ها بر دمید). بر before دمید gives a sense of upward/onward motion ('blew up' or 'surged').
segments.s003-g28: سر + رستنی is a noun phrase 'the head/top of the plants'; سوی بالا is an adverbial phrase 'toward above/upward'; کشید is the past of کشیدن ('stretched/drew/reached'), so the whole clause means the plant-tops stretched up. Note: the lemma/senses provided for کشید do not include 'to draw/stretch', so the correct sense is marked separately below.
segments.s003-g29: زمین را uses را to mark the topical/definite noun. The phrase بلندی نبد جایگاه means 'there was no high place'—نبد is an older negative copula ('was not / did not exist') following the noun, so the clause denies the existence of any elevation or high seat.
segments.s003-g30: یکی is an indefinite 'one / a certain' and مرکزی qualifies it ('a central'). تیره and سیاه are coordinate adjectives describing that place; بود is the past copula 'was' placed after the adjectives (typical word order in older Persian).
segments.s003-g31: ستاره is the subject; بر + او means 'over/on it' (referring back to the dark central spot). برشگفتی is an adverbial/nominal phrase 'with great wonder' or 'as a great marvel'; نمود is the past of نمودن used in the sense 'appeared / showed itself.'
segments.s003-g32: به خاک اندرون is a locative phrase 'into/within the inner soil/earth' (به + noun + اندرون = 'into the interior'); روشنایی is the subject ('brightness, light') and فزود (past of فزودن) means 'increased' or 'grew'. The verb comes at the end in typical classical order.
segments.s003-g33: همی is the continuous/adverbial marker ('kept on' / 'still') placed before the verbal phrase. بر شد is a compound: بر (up/on) + شد (past of شدن) = 'rose up'. فرود آمد is another verbal phrase 'descended/ came down' but is placed before its patient/subject آب (poetic inversion: verb then noun). The sequence therefore states two simultaneous actions: fire rose and water descended.
segments.s003-g34: همی again marks continuous action. گشت here means 'turned' (intransitive 'to turn, go round'); گرد زمین is a prepositional phrase 'around the earth' (گرد = around + زمین = earth). آفتاب (sun) appears at the end due to poetic word order; the clause reads 'the sun kept turning around the earth.'
segments.s003-g35: گیا (vegetation, plants) is the subject and رست (past of رستن) is the verb 'sprang up / grew'. با + چند گونه درخت is an accompanying/instrumental phrase 'with several kinds of trees' (چند = several; گونه = kinds; درخت = trees). Word order is subject–verb, then a prepositional accompaniment.
segments.s003-g36: This line uses inverted/poetic order: به زیر اندر آمد = 'came under / came down beneath' (به ... اندر آمد = 'was brought into / came into a lower position') with سران‌شان ('their tops/heads') following as the affected theme. ز بخت is an ablative/casual phrase 'because of / by fate' (ز = by/from; بخت = fate). The whole clause reads 'their tops were brought low by fate' (interpretation: their crowns/tops came under/bowed because of fate).
segments.s003-g37: ببالد is an older/poetic present infinitive/finite form meaning 'takes pride / boasts'; ندارد negates it ('has not/does not have'), so the phrase means 'has no pride except...'. جز این نیرویی = 'except this force/strength'. Literal order (verb before ندارد) is characteristic of classical Persian style.
segments.s003-g38: نپوید is a negative present/subjunctive form 'does not pursue/seek'. چو introduces a comparison 'as/like', and پویندگان هر سویی = 'those who roam/seek in every direction' (هر سویی = every direction). Together: 'it does not go about seeking in every direction like wanderers.'
segments.s003-g39: و زان پس = 'and after that/thereafter'. چو here means 'when' (temporal). جنبنده آمد پدید is a two-word verbal compound with slight inversion: جنبنده = 'a living/moving creature' and آمد پدید = 'came into being / appeared' (poetic order; more commonly پدید آمد).
segments.s003-g40: همه رستنی = 'all the vegetation' (subject). زیر خویش آورید is a causative/active past meaning 'brought (it) under themselves' (زیر = under; خویش = their own; آورید = brought). In context the implied agent of آورید is the living creatures: when creatures appeared, they made the plants subordinate/under their control or beneath them (literal: brought under themselves).
segments.s003-g41: A list of three objects (خور, خواب, آرام) is placed before the verb جوید. The verb جوید is a present/habitual form meaning “seeks”; the enclitic/adverb همی after the verb marks continuous or habitual action. The subject is omitted (third person singular) as is common in Persian poetry.
segments.s003-g42: و begins the clause as a simple coordinating conjunction. زان here links back to the previous clause and functions as 'for that' / 'therefore'. زندگی کام is a noun + noun combination where کام (here) carries the sense 'satisfaction/fulfillment' and is the object of جوید; the verb and final همی repeat the habitual present construction.
segments.s003-g43: The correlative negation نه ... و نه marks two parallel lacks: گویا زبان (an 'eloquent/speaking tongue') and جویا خرد ('a seeking/inquisitive wisdom/mind'). Adjective-like words (گویا, جویا) precede the nouns they modify, a poetic word order; the whole phrase is a nominal clause describing absence of speech and intellect.
segments.s003-g44: ز (poetic form of از) introduces sources: 'from earth and from chaff.' The repetition of ز before the second item is a common poetic emphasis. تن پرورد is a compact nominal phrase: تن (body) + پرورد (past/participle of پروردن ‘to nurture’), i.e. 'a body nourished/formed from...'; the verb/copula is omitted as is usual in descriptive clauses.
segments.s003-g45: نداند is the negative present of دانستن ('does not know') with an omitted subject (third person). بد و نیک‌فرجام are coordinated adjectives modifying کار; نیک‌فرجام is a compound adjective meaning 'with a good/fortunate end.' Word order is verb first (negation) then the predicate (adjectives + noun).
segments.s003-g46: النَهِی (نخواهد) here expresses lack of will/volition or future negation ('will not' / 'does not wish'). The phrase is arranged poetically: the logical object 'بندگی کردگار' (service/devotion to the Creator) is present, and از او indicates the agent/ source ('from him' / 'by him') — in other words, 'servitude to the Creator will not come from him' (he will not render such service).
segments.s003-g47: چو here means 'since' or 'as' and introduces a reason clause; the following sequence (دانا توانا بد و دادگر) is a string of adjectives describing an implied subject (carrying over from the previous clause, e.g. the Creator). Adjectives are listed in sequence and coordinated by و; poetic order omits an explicit copula.
segments.s003-g48: از here functions as a causal/prepositional link 'because of / on account of.' ایرا is an archaic/obscure deictic reference ('this' / 'because of that'); نکرد is the past negative 'did not make/do'; ایچ (nothing) + پنهان (hidden) + هنر (art/skill) form the object: 'he did not make any art hidden.' The clause thus states that for that reason no skill was concealed.
segments.s003-g17: پدید آمد is a two‑word verb phrase meaning “appeared / came into view” (پدید + آمد) with the verb in the past tense. The following noun phrase این گنبد تیز رو (this dome, literally “this dome sharp‑faced”) places the demonstrative این before the noun and uses the adjective تیز with رو as a postpositive element meaning “‑faced” or “in appearance” (a poetic compound). Word order is somewhat inverted for effect (verb first, then the subject/phrase).
segments.s003-g18: شگفتی نماینده is a compound noun phrase: شگفتی (a marvel/wonder) with نماینده as an agentive noun meaning “one who shows / displays” (i.e., “a marvel‑displayer” or “a show of wonders”). نو به نو is an adverbial phrase “new by new,” meaning “anew” or “again and again” — به here functions adverbially ("by/‑ly") linking the two نو elements to give the iterative sense.
segments.s003-g19: Grammatically this is a past‑tense clause where شد is the copular/auxiliary verb “became.” The words before it name the subject(s) (here written as ابر ده و دو هفت in the running text) and کدخدای is the predicate noun ("kadkhodā / village‑headman"). The coordinating و links items in the subject; the overall sense is that those named entities “became” kadhkhodāys.
segments.s003-g20: گرفتند is past 3rd‑person plural “they took/assumed.” هر یک = “each one” specifies distribution among them, and سزاوار جای is a noun phrase where سزاوار (adjective) modifies جای to mean “a place befitting/deserving” — so the clause literally says “they took, each one, a place worthy (of him).” Word order (verb before modifiers) reflects a poetic, somewhat inverted order.
segments.s003-g21: در بخشش و دادن is a locative/prepositional phrase (در + two gerund/noun forms) meaning “in distribution and giving.” آمد پدید is the same verb pair as above (here in the order آمد پدید for poetic variation) meaning “appeared / came into being.” The whole segment says that generosity/bounty came forth in the act of giving.
segments.s003-g22: ببخشید is a past‑tense verb meaning “he bestowed / gave away” (here used in narrative past). دانا (the wise one) follows the verb (inverted order typical of poetry) as the agent. چنان چون = “in such a way as” or “just as,” and سزید is a past/passive‑like verbal adjective from سزایدن meaning “(as) was deserved / fitting.” Together: “the wise bestowed as was fitting.”
segments.s003-g23: فلک‌ها is the plural subject “the heavens/skies.” یک اندر دگر is an adverbial phrase meaning “one into/within another” or “one upon the other,” and بسته شد is a past passive/inchoative construction “were closed / became closed.” The segment thus describes the skies being shut together in succession — a compact poetic expression.
segments.s003-g24: بجنبید here is past (they/it) “moved” or “was set in motion” (the be‑prefixed past form common in classical/poetic Persian). چون introduces a temporal/causal clause “when/as,” and کار پیوسته شد is “the work became continuous / the affair was made settled.” So the clause says that things moved as the work was brought to continuity/completion.

sentences.s003-s5: At first, when the fire breathed into motion, from its warmth dryness then appeared.
sentences.s003-s6: And thereafter a gentle coldness appeared; from that coldness the same openness increased.
sentences.s003-s7: When these four essences came into place, they came for the sake of a spongy abode.
sentences.s003-s8: The essences were made one within another; from every kind a raised neck stood aloft.
sentences.s003-s1: From the beginning you must rightly know the prime matter of the essences.
sentences.s003-s2: Know that God created a thing out of nothing, so that he might bring forth power.
sentences.s003-s3: These four are the prime matters of the essences, brought forth without toil and without misfortune.
sentences.s003-s4: One fire arose, resplendent and shining, between water and wind upon the dark earth.
sentences.s003-s25: Thus is the outcome of the world's affairs; no one knows the manifest and the hidden.
sentences.s003-s13: When the earth became like the sea, like a mountain, like a plain, and like longing, it assumed the semblance of a bright lamp.
sentences.s003-s14: The mountain swelled and the waters blew up; the tops of the vegetation reached upward.
sentences.s003-s15: The earth had no height or elevated place; there was a single central spot, dark and black.
sentences.s003-s16: A star appeared above it in wonder; brightness increased within the inner earth.
sentences.s003-s17: Fire rose up, water came down, and the sun kept turning around the earth.
sentences.s003-s18: Vegetation sprang up, with several kinds of trees; by fate their tops were brought low.
sentences.s003-s19: It takes pride in nothing but this force; it does not seek out every direction like wanderers.
sentences.s003-s20: And thereafter, when a living creature appeared, they brought all the vegetation under themselves.
sentences.s003-s21: He seeks food, sleep, and rest, and for that he seeks the fulfillment of life.
sentences.s003-s22: He has neither a speaking tongue nor an inquiring mind; his body is nourished from earth and from chaff.
sentences.s003-s23: He knows not the bad or good outcome of deeds; service to the Creator will not come from him.
sentences.s003-s24: Since (he is) wise, powerful, good and just, on that account he did not hide any art/skill.
sentences.s003-s9: This sharp‑faced dome appeared, displaying marvels anew.
sentences.s003-s10: Abar‑deh and Do‑haft became kadkhodāys; each took a fitting place.
sentences.s003-s11: In giving and distributing, bounty appeared; the wise bestowed (gifts) as was fitting.
sentences.s003-s12: The heavens were closed one within another; things moved when the work became continuous.
